content_standards.description: The learners should have knowledge and understanding of parts of a circle, including circumference.

performance_standards.description: The learners are able to describe the parts of a circle and determine its circumference.

competencies.description: Draw circles with different radii using a pair of compasses.
Identify and describe the parts of a circle.
Measure the circumference of circles using appropriate tools.
Approximate the value of pi (π), the ratio of the circumference to the diameter.
Find the circumference of a circle using C = πd or C = 2πr.

content_standards.description: The learners should have knowledge and understanding of area of a circle.

performance_standards.description: The learners are able to determine the area of a circle and solve related problems.

competencies.description: Explore inductively the area of a circle leading to the formula A = πr².
Find the area of a circle using the formula.
Solve problems involving the circumference and area of circles.

content_standards.description: The learners should have knowledge and understanding of composite figures composed of any two or more of: triangle, square, rectangle, circle, and semi-circle.

performance_standards.description: The learners are able to determine the area of composite figures and solve related problems.

competencies.description: Find the area of composite figures composed of any two or more of the following: triangle, square, rectangle, circle, and semicircle.
Solve problems involving circumference and area of composite figures.

content_standards.description: The learners should have knowledge and understanding of pie graphs.

performance_standards.description: The learners are able to construct, interpret, and analyze pie graphs.

competencies.description: Find the angle measures and/or percentages based on the given data for a pie graph.
Construct a pie graph using appropriate tools.
Interpret data presented in a pie graph.
Interpret data from digital media that are presented in tabular or graphical form.
Draw conclusions or make inferences based on data presented in a pie graph.
Solve problems using data presented in a pie graph.

content_standards.description: The learners should have knowledge and understanding of common factors, greatest common factors, common multiples, and least common multiples.

performance_standards.description: The learners are able to determine common factors, greatest common factors (GCF), common multiples, least common multiples (LCM), and solve related problems.

competencies.description: Determine the common factors and the greatest common factor (GCF) of two numbers using listing, prime factorization, and continuous division.
Find the common multiples and least common multiple (LCM) of two numbers using listing, prime factorization, and continuous division.
Solve problems involving GCF and LCM.
